reasoning: AICO (Autoridades Indígenas de Colombia) is a minor political party that represents indigenous communities. In the Colombian Senate (108 seats), AICO historically competes for the special indigenous constituency, typically winning only 1 seat (as seen in 2014, 2018, and 2022). Major political parties and coalitions like the Historic Pact (Pacto Histórico), Conservative Party, Liberal Party, Democratic Center, and others regularly win between 10 and 20 seats. There is no historical precedent or major current political realignment that would allow a small indigenous party to suddenly win the plurality of seats in the national Senate. It is practically impossible for them to be the largest party.
